meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: A Student Checklist for Faster Coding
content.seo_description: Stuck on code? Master debugging with AI using this expert-led checklist. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your development journey.
content.answer_block: Debugging is the primary hurdle for every developer. By integrating AI-assisted debugging, you transform your workflow from trial-and-error to systematic resolution. Use Antigravity for rapid architectural insights and Claude Code for CLI-based deep dives. This approach slashes downtime, deepens your technical understanding, and accelerates your path to production.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your code remains stubbornly broken. You have spent hours chasing a phantom bug, and the frustration is mounting. This is the 'developer wall'—a rite of passage that feels like a dead end but is actually the most critical learning moment of your career.

But the rules of the game have changed. You no longer need to suffer in silence or scan forums for hours. By leveraging AI agents, you can treat your IDE like a senior mentor available 24/7. This checklist is your blueprint to turn those red error logs into successful commits and finally ship your first real-world project.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Before throwing code at an AI, you must narrow the scope. Identify exactly which function or API call is returning the unexpected output.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following function. Identify the exact line where the logic deviates from the intended outcome and explain why it fails.

steps.title: Contextualize the Environment
steps.description: AI needs context to be effective. Provide the relevant dependency versions and the surrounding file structure to avoid hallucinated solutions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Considering my project environment (Node.js 18, React 18), why would this specific function cause a runtime type error?

steps.title: Execute Automated Tracebacks
steps.description: Use Claude Code to step through the execution flow. This helps you visualize how data moves through your application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Trace the data flow for this variable from initialization to the point of failure. Where does the mutation go wrong?

steps.title: Iterative Refactoring
steps.description: Apply the fix provided by the AI, but verify it against edge cases to ensure the solution is robust and not just a patch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this code to handle empty state and null values, ensuring it follows modern industry best practices.

steps.title: Verify with Unit Testing
steps.description: Never trust code you haven't tested. Use AI to generate a regression test that ensures the bug never returns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite that covers the original bug and two edge cases to ensure long-term stability.

pro_tips: Always ask the AI to 'explain like I am a junior engineer' to force it to break down complex architectural patterns into understandable logic.
Paste your error logs directly into Claude Code; it is trained to recognize stack trace patterns faster than a human grepping logs.
Use Antigravity to generate 'pseudo-code' first; if the logic holds in plain English, the implementation is usually a minor syntax hurdle.
If the AI provides a solution you don't understand, ask it to 'write a comment for every line of this fix' to ensure you are learning, not just copy-pasting.
When debugging asynchronous code, explicitly mention the event loop or promise chain to the AI to get more accurate concurrency-related fixes.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your current project, commit your changes to GitHub, write a brief 'Post-Mortem' documentation file explaining what you learned, and start planning your next feature set.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the tools to conquer any error log that stands in your way. Stop fearing the debugger and start mastering your craft. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
